message:
fix(share): reject symlinks across all filesystem operations to prevent path traversal attacks

Multiple paths in the share crate used .is_dir()/.is_file()/.exists()
which follow symlinks, creating a potential vector for symlink-based
path traversal where a malicious symlink could redirect bundle packaging,
deployment artifact validation, or project detection to arbitrary
filesystem locations.

- bundle.rs: propagate errors from ensure_real_directory on attachments
  dir; replace .is_dir()/.is_file() with is_real_dir/is_real_file in
  ZIP packaging, viewer asset copying, and SRI computation; add 5 tests
  verifying symlink rejection
- deploy.rs: sort chunk artifact validation entries for deterministic
  output; add 1 test for ordering stability
- detection.rs: use symlink_metadata instead of .exists() in
  find_ancestor_path; filter out symlinked files/dirs in all directory
  scans (workflows, scripts, docs) and sort entries for deterministic
  output; add 10 tests for symlink rejection and ordering
- hosting.rs: apply symlink-safe pattern to find_ancestor_path, workflow
  scanning, and S3 script detection; add 4 tests
- planner.rs: use is_real_file/is_real_dir instead of .exists()/.is_dir()
  in has_strong_project_root_marker; ignore symlinked scripts/ dirs in
  resolve_detection_root; add 2 tests
- executor.rs: add ensure_real_directory() that walks each path component
  rejecting symlinks for mkdir -p emulation; add write_generated_file()
  that refuses symlinked targets; add 3 tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
